rulings.Case Title: STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND VS. ONVOI BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT SELECT PERSONNEL SERVICES, INC DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled, factual issue whether claims are time barred. (See allegations in paragraphs 44-60 and Livette vs. FC Financial Assoc. (1981) 124 Cal.App.3d 413.) Ten days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARIN MANAGEMENT, INC VS. PANKAJ K PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Deault Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT PANKAJ PATEL Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment. Motion granted. Defendants not properly served and proposed answer is provided. Any writs of execution based upon the judgment are quashed. Answer to be filed within two court days from the date of the order. Request for attorney fees and costs are denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ZIZI HAWTHORNE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF ZIZI HAWTHORNE Motion to be relieved as counsel. Motion is granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SHERRY LEE VS. GUO WEI DENG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 4, PLAINTIFF SHERRY LEE MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Off Calendar, matter to be re-calendared in Department 301 because the case number is odd. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PROSPERO LAGLEVA VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT OMRI BERGER MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY CURTIN et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiffs do not allege that defendant breached the loan agreement and allege no basis for a breach of the duty of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend their complaint to allege additional causes of action supported by a legal and factual basis. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J.MARYMONT, INC., DBA CORPORATE SEARCH, A VS. QUARK PHARMACEUTICALS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT QUARK PHARMACEUTICALS, INC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, amended complaint filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J.MARYMONT, INC., DBA CORPORATE SEARCH, A VS. QUARK PHARMACEUTICALS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT QUARK PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, amended complaint filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HOY PING BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION VS. ROBERT FEKONG MAUK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 10, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT JASON YOUNG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with five days leave to amend to plead that the premises are commercial and exempt from the just cause eviction requirements of the San Francisco Administrative Code. =(302/CWW)
